name: Christian Family
description_short: Christian Family Credit Union is a faith-based, member-owned credit union offering savings, checking, loans, and mortgages with a mission focused on Kingdom building and financial stewardship.
description_long:
Christian Family Credit Union is a faith-based, not-for-profit credit union serving Christians and Christian families seeking financial services aligned with their values. Founded on principles of integrity, honesty, and glorifying God, the organization positions itself as more than a typical financial institution—framing banking as an act of Kingdom building. The credit union serves individual members, churches, and businesses, with membership eligibility requirements tied to faith community affiliation. Members can join individually or through their church.

Christian Family CU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, share certificates, personal loans, home equity loans, mortgages, vehicle loans, adoption loans, and church financing services. They provide online and mobile banking platforms, VISA credit cards, and TruStage Insurance products. Loan offerings include options specifically marketed toward Christian life events (adoption, church ministry funding, education). The institution emphasizes above-market interest rates on savings and below-market rates on loans as a benefit of membership.

What distinguishes Christian Family CU is its explicit Christian mission and values-based lending philosophy. Unlike secular credit unions, this organization frames financial transactions as ministry, with member deposits directly funding loans to other Christians and local church building projects. They market specialized loan products like adoption loans and church financing that reflect their faith focus. The organization maintains a Statement of Faith and Guiding Principles on their website, making their religious orientation transparent and central to their identity.

Christian Family CU operates as a legitimate NCUA-insured credit union with standard regulatory oversight. However, prospective members must be aware that membership eligibility is restricted to those within their defined Christian community—this is not an open-access financial institution. The website provides limited detail on specific rates, fees, or membership eligibility criteria, requiring direct contact for detailed information. Like all credit unions, they offer the member-ownership structure and potential yield advantages of cooperative banking, but the faith-based restriction may limit accessibility for some consumers.
services: Vehicle loans (auto, motorcycle, RV, marine, powersport)
Church and ministry building project financing
Check imaging and cleared check verification
pros: NCUA-insured member deposits with standard credit union safety protections
Specialized loan products for faith-based life events (adoption loans, church building financing)
Advertises above-market savings rates and below-market loan rates as member benefits
Online and mobile banking platform for account management 24/7
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits can be returned to members
VISA credit card and TruStage Insurance products available
Check imaging and transaction verification available through online banking
cons: Membership restricted to Christian individuals, families, and churches—not universally accessible
Website provides no specific rate information, requiring members to contact directly for quotes
Limited branch/ATM details provided on website; geographic service area unclear
No information about fees, minimum balances, or account requirements disclosed
Heavy religious messaging may not appeal to secular consumers or those of other faiths
best_for: Christians and faith-based families seeking banking aligned with their religious values
Church organizations and ministries needing business banking and building project financing
Families pursuing adoptions who want specialized adoption loan products
Member-owned financial institution advocates who prefer cooperative banking over for-profit banks
diagnosis: Christian Family CU is best for practicing Christians and faith-based organizations seeking full-service banking with values alignment, but membership is restricted to those within their Christian community—making it inaccessible to non-Christian consumers. The main caveat is that specific rates, fees, and detailed eligibility criteria are not published on their website, requiring direct contact before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
